events.title: Cars & Coffee at The Throttlestop
events.description: Start Your Engines: Cars & Coffee at The Throttlestop Presented by Wegner Automotive Prepare for the most electric season in Throttlestop history. This year, Throttlestop is driving change with their new “Passion for Purpose” initiative, proudly partnering with The United Way of Sheboygan County to give back to the Elkhart Lake community. Throttlestop has assembled an elite lineup of sponsors to make this year better than ever and are thrilled to welcome Wegner Automotive , curators of some of the baddest racing engines on the planet, as the title sponsor. Alongside them, West Bend Dyno and Milwaukee Tool will be on-site with massive displays and some of the most exciting new builds in the country. The Details: When: Sat. June 13th, 2026 8:00 AM to 11:00 AM The Vibe: Jaw-dropping revs, breathtaking displays, and great conversation. Food: Fresh donuts from Johnston’s Bakery . Fuel: Stone Creek Coffee , provided by West Bend Dyno . Cost: Free for the whole family! Whether you are here for the precision of a Wegner-built powerhouse or the latest gear from Milwaukee Tool, you won’t want to miss this. Grab a coffee, bring the family, and talk shop. See you at the showroom!...
events.location: Throttlestop, 20 Victory Lane, Elkhart Lake, WI

events.title: Gyotaku Lake Fish Printing Workshop at The Mad Patcher
events.description: Head over to The Mad Patcher for a unique, hands-on art experience inspired by a centuries-old Japanese tradition. During the Elkhart Lake 4th of July Parade, guests are invited to participate in guided fish printing using locally caught Wisconsin lake fish on T-shirts, paper and patches. All skill levels are welcome, and participants can choose from a wide variety of colors and gradients to create their own one-of-a-kind print. Known as Gyotaku , or “fish rubbing,” this traditional printing technique originated in Japan during the Edo Period (1603–1867). Japanese fishermen developed the method as a way to document and preserve the size, species, and intricate details of their catches long before the invention of photography. The prints also served as records for fishing competitions while allowing the fish to still be used for food. Today, Gyotaku remains a distinctive art form practiced by artists and fishing enthusiasts around the world. This interactive demonstration offers a rare opportunity to learn about the history of the craft while creating a memorable piece of art inspired by Wisconsin’s local waters. Reserve Your Spot...
events.location: The Mad Patcher, 80 Square Street, Elkhart Lake, WI

events.title: Elkhart Lake Fireman’s 4th of July Parade
events.description: Elkhart Lake Fire Department’s Annual 4th of July Parade is on Sunday, July 5th, 2026 at 11:30am . There will also be a special appearance by the Wisconsin Badger Band! The route begins on East Street by Siebkens Resort and will end at Fireman’s Park. Afterwards head over to Fireman’s Park for food, drinks, live music and a 5th Quarter Show by the Wisconsin Badger Band. Parade participants are not required to pre-register for the parade. events.title: Japanese Wood Block Printing Workshop at The Mad Patcher
events.description: Discover the art of wood block printing in this hands-on workshop where participants will carve their own custom designs and print them onto paper using naturally crafted dyes. All experience levels are welcome, and instructors will provide guidance throughout the process. Due to the use of sharp carving tools, participants must be at least 14 years old. All materials, including wood blocks, tools, and printing supplies, will be provided. Guests are also welcome to bring their own items, such as fabric or tote bags, to print on. Wood block printing is one of the oldest forms of printmaking, dating back hundreds of years and helping pave the way for modern printing techniques. From European botanical prints to Japan’s renowned Ukiyo-e artwork, the craft has played an important role in sharing art and stories across generations. Participants will leave with their hand-carved wood block to continue creating prints at home. Reserve Your Spot...
events.location: The Mad Patcher, 80 Square Street, Elkhart Lake, WI

Important All attendees must complete the online SpeedWaiver prior to arrival. Your completed waiver will serve as your admission into the event.
events.location: Road America, Elkhart Lake, WI

events.title: Sew A Sock Monkey Workshop at The Mad Patcher
events.description: Create your very own personalized sock monkey in this fun and family-friendly workshop held on the platform of Elkhart Lake’s Historic Depot. Participants will be guided through the process of hand sewing a custom sock monkey using socks with imperfect knits donated from Wigwam’s manufacturing process. Led by Mad Patcher founder Baxter Salzwedel, this workshop is perfect for refreshing hand-sewing skills while creating a unique handmade keepsake. All ages and skill levels are welcome, and participants are encouraged to bring socks with special colors or designs if they would like to incorporate them into their project. Attendees will leave with an adorable custom sock monkey and a memorable handcrafted creation to take home. Reserve Your Spot...
events.location: The Mad Patcher, 80 Square Street, Elkhart Lake, WI

events.title: 4 Miles of Fitness
events.description: Bike, walk, or run your way around Road America’s legendary 4-mile, 14-turn road course during this fan-favorite fitness series. Set against the scenic 640-acre property, participants can explore everything from wooded cross-country trails to service roads and even the iconic racing surface itself, all in a safe, traffic-free environment. Dates & Times: Mondays & Wednesdays | May 4 – September 30, 2026 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM (September hours: 5:30 PM – 7:30 PM) Pricing: $5 per person (cash only at the gate) $25 for an 8-visit pass $60 for an unlimited season membership Youth 16 and under are free with a paid adult. Group discounts available for groups of 10 or more. Know Before You Go: All participants must complete the SpeedWaiver before arrival. For safety, pets, scooters, and skateboards are not permitted. Lace up, roll out and experience Road America like never before—no engines required....
